discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape with semis (SMH +5.2%) and tech (XLK +2.7%) leading; energy (XLE -4.1%) the lone weak sector as oil retraces despite Iran tension headlines. Single dramatic mid-cap dislocation: PRIM down 50% on earnings + officer departure 8-K, requires careful read.

notable_index_moves: SMH +5.2% and TSM +6.4% on continued AI/semi rally; NVDA +5.8%
XLE -4.1% as oil pulls back despite Iran headlines — energy weakness diverges from broad tape
XLU -1.4% — defensive rotation out as risk appetite returns
VIX only 17.5 despite geopolitical backdrop suggests complacency or hedge unwind

discoveries.name: Primoris Services Corporation
discoveries.mechanism: Earnings (2.02) combined with officer departure (5.02) on same 8-K is a classic compounding-bad-news pattern; -50% on a $5.5B engineering & construction name implies either a severe guidance cut, material project write-down, or accounting concern alongside management exit. Magnitude is extreme even for bad earnings — suggests the 5.02 may involve CFO or accounting-related departure, which markets price more harshly than the headline numbers alone justify. However, without confirmation of a 4.02 (restatement), a 50% move on an industrial services name with multi-year backlog is likely overshooting fundamentals — but we cannot confirm overdone without reading the filing.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Combination of 2.02 + 5.02 on same filing day with -50% reaction and -39% over five days suggests pre-leak followed by confirmation; severity of move points to materially negative guidance alongside management change rather than a routine retirement.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K directly — identify WHICH officer departed (CFO vs other) and whether departure language is 'retirement', 'mutual agreement', or 'effective immediately'
Compare reported Q1 numbers and any revised FY2026 guidance to prior consensus and prior guide — quantify the cut
Check whether backlog figures were disclosed and if any specific large projects were written down or cancelled (utility, renewables, or pipeline segments)
Look for any mention of internal controls, audit committee review, or auditor consultation language — these would escalate to 4.02 risk
Check peer reactions (MTZ, MYRG, EME, FIX) — sympathy moves would indicate sector read-across; isolated PRIM move suggests company-specific
Review short interest and options activity pre-print for evidence of leak
Assess balance sheet: debt covenants, liquidity — engineering firms with project losses can hit covenants fast
discoveries.what_would_falsify: If 8-K reveals (a) a 4.02 non-reliance / restatement, (b) CFO departure 'effective immediately' with no successor, or (c) covenant breach — then -50% is RATIONAL not overdone. Conversely, if it's planned CEO retirement plus a mid-single-digit guide cut, then -50% is LIKELY OVERDONE.

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Oil pulls back despite Iran tension; XLE -4.1% diverges from risk-on tape

discovery.no_signals_note: Only one mid-cap mover provided (PRIM). Worth noting: the Fastly headline (earnings beat + raised guidance + stock down) is the kind of misread-of-guidance setup we look for, but Fastly was not in the movers list and we lack the magnitude data to evaluate. Worth Michael's attention as a separate research item.

ai_announcements.headline: Multiple AI industry items (Samsung $1T, SpaceX Terafab, DeepSeek $45B, Snap-Perplexity breakup, Apple Siri settlement, Match AI hiring slowdown, Google AI search w/ Reddit)
ai_announcements.summary: Mixed bag of AI ecosystem news. Nothing in the news flow plausibly explains a -50% move in Primoris Services, an engineering/construction firm. PRIM filed an 8-K on 2026-05-05 with earnings (2.02) and officer departure (5.02) items — that is the overwhelmingly likely catalyst, not AI news. Note: PRIM has data center / power infrastructure exposure (utility & energy services), so AI-capex narratives could matter at the margin, but a -50% single-day move is earnings/guidance/management driven.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: PRIM builds power/utility/data-center infrastructure; AI capex boom (Samsung $1T, SpaceX Terafab $119B, neocloud buildouts) is a TAILWIND, not a threat. AI announcements in this batch do not justify any selloff in PRIM.
research_pointers: Read the 5/5 8-K and Q1 earnings release — items 2.02 (earnings) + 5.02 (officer change) are the actual catalyst, not AI news
Check if guidance was cut on Utilities or Energy segment, or if a major customer (data center / pipeline) project was canceled
Determine which officer departed (CEO/CFO departure would explain magnitude)
Confirm data-center / power-infra backlog commentary — if backlog intact, -50% on earnings miss + exec exit may be overdone vs. AI-infra structural demand
FALSIFIER: if 8-K reveals accounting restatement, fraud, material backlog cancellation, or CEO termination for cause, the -50% is rational and not an opportunity

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: PRIM's -50% move is almost certainly earnings + 5.02 officer departure driven, not AI-news driven. No clean AI-mispricing setup in tonight's flow. Other AI headlines (Samsung $1T, SpaceX Terafab, Apple settlement, Snap-Perplexity unwind) are largely confirmatory/known and not generating obvious sympathy dislocations in the provided market data.
